author: Gustavo de Aguiar Martins

interpretation.legacy_rule: Legacy weak-authority strong decisions are preserved as warnings so historical evidence is not rewritten.
interpretation.native_rule: Native events must carry H2/H3 authority for collapse, block, prove or reclassify decisions.
interpretation.sovereignty: Authority, entity, proof and relation policies are checked before an event is treated as production-grade evidence.

policy.H0: Observation only; strong decisions are warnings only for legacy evidence when legacy_tolerant is true.
policy.H1: Advisory signal or memory; not enough alone for new strong actions.
policy.H2: Guarded threshold, routing, native emission or runtime action.

recommendations: Move new runtime strong decisions to native pnva.event.v1 emission with H2/H3 authority.
Keep legacy low-authority strong decisions visible as warnings until the source runtime is upgraded.
Require etev_guard on every ETEV_GUARD_PASS and ETEV_GUARD_BLOCK event.
Require valid proof_hash and proof_ref for every collapse, block, prove or reclassify decision.
